app.name: Northstar DISC
app.description: A premium, practical DISC reflection designed for leaders, teams, and thoughtful professionals.

launch.summary: A practical, elegant reflection that helps you understand your communication style with clarity.
launch.subtitle: A calm, guided check-in that helps you see how you naturally move through work and relationships.
launch.note: Three thoughtful prompts. No signup. No pressure.

status.shareHint: Works beautifully for LinkedIn-style sharing and quick offline export.

quiz.resultPlaceholder: We are preparing your profile now.

questions.prompt: 1. When building momentum on a new project, I tend to...

options.label: Move quickly and decisively to hit ambitious targets
Bring warmth, vision, and high energy to the team
Create steady calm, harmony, and consistency
Refine details, set standards, and ensure accuracy

questions.prompt: 2. In a group setting, I feel most useful when I...

options.label: Set clear direction and take charge of decisions
Connect people, inspire ideas, and spark conversation
Support team members patiently and listen deeply
Organize complex information and provide logical structure

questions.prompt: 3. When making important decisions, I place the highest value on...

options.label: Speed, autonomy, and direct impact on the bottom line
Buy-in, enthusiasm, and how it affects morale
Consensus, stability, and minimizing disruption
Data, logic, risk assessment, and thorough facts

questions.prompt: 4. My natural speed of execution is usually...

options.label: Urgent and fast-paced; I dislike waiting around
Spontaneous and energetic, driven by momentum
Paced and deliberate; I prefer a steady flow
Methodical and careful; quality takes priority over speed

questions.prompt: 5. When given a complex problem with vague guidelines, I instinctively...

options.label: Take ownership immediately and forge my own solution path
Brainstorm out loud with others to find creative angles
Seek alignment with the team before taking step one
Gather all available facts to build a systematic plan

questions.prompt: 6. When delegating tasks to others, my priority is ensuring...

options.label: They focus entirely on hitting the desired outcome quickly
They feel empowered, excited, and inspired to do it
They have the exact support and time they need to succeed
They have precise instructions, rules, and quality standards

questions.prompt: 7. My reaction to strict rules and procedures is typically...

options.label: To bypass them if they slow down progress or results
To treat them as flexible guidelines rather than rigid limits
To respect them because they provide safety and structure
To follow and enforce them strictly to prevent mistakes

questions.prompt: 8. In a high-stakes meeting, you will most likely see me...

options.label: Challenging assumptions and driving straight to action points
Pitching bold ideas and keeping energy levels high
Listening attentiveness and encouraging quieter voices
Taking detailed notes, asking probing questions, and verifying claims

questions.prompt: 9. How do you feel about taking calculated risks?

options.label: I embrace them boldly if the potential reward is high
I jump in enthusiastically if it feels exciting and fresh
I prefer safe, proven routes with minimal uncertainty
I analyze all probabilities deeply before taking a single step

questions.prompt: 10. When starting my work day, I prefer to focus first on...

options.label: The highest-impact task that moves the needle right away
Checking in with people and connecting with the team
Continuing routine tasks in a steady, predictable order
Reviewing my schedule, organized lists, and priority details

questions.prompt: 11. In daily communication, my natural style is...

options.label: Direct, brief, and focused purely on outcomes
Animated, expressive, and story-driven
Warm, patient, and focused on active listening
Precise, objective, and backed by evidence

questions.prompt: 12. When building working relationships, I place the most trust in people who are...

options.label: Competent, decisive, and accountable for results
Open-minded, engaging, and positive-minded
Loyal, consistent, and genuinely supportive
Thorough, honest, and rigorous in their standards

questions.prompt: 13. My approach to networking and meeting new colleagues is...

options.label: Strategic; I look for connections that drive key objectives
Effortless; I love engaging people and sharing energy
Quietly observant; I prefer deep 1-on-1 connections over time
Reserved; I engage when there is a clear, meaningful reason

questions.prompt: 14. When giving feedback to someone, I tend to be...

options.label: Blunt, frank, and focused on fixing issues instantly
Encouraging, optimistic, and focused on potential
Gentle, tactful, and careful not to hurt feelings
Specific, objective, and focused on facts and errors

questions.prompt: 15. People who know me well would describe my social presence as...

options.label: Assertive, strong-willed, and commanding
Charismatic, persuasive, and outgoing
Kind, reliable, and approachable
Discreet, analytical, and thoughtful

questions.prompt: 16. In conversations, I tend to get frustrated most by...

options.label: Indecisiveness, hesitation, and endless debate
Pessimism, overly rigid rules, and dull detail-checking
Aggressive tone, confrontation, and sudden changes
Vague statements, emotional arguments, and lack of proof

questions.prompt: 17. When persuading others to adopt my view, I rely heavily on...

options.label: Confident assertion and pointing out the clear advantages
Enthusiasm, storytelling, and painting a compelling vision
Showing how it benefits the group and keeps peace
Presenting structured logic, data, and well-researched facts

questions.prompt: 18. When working in cross-functional teams, my role often becomes...

options.label: The driver pushing everyone across the finish line
The spark connecting ideas and keeping spirits high
The anchor keeping everyone grounded and collaborating
The reviewer ensuring quality control and exact precision

questions.prompt: 19. How do you handle emotional expressions from teammates?

options.label: I redirect focus back to the facts and required actions
I empathize quickly and try to brighten the mood
I offer a quiet, patient ear and a supportive presence
I analyze the situation logically to help solve the root issue

questions.prompt: 20. When receiving praise, I feel most appreciated when acknowledged for my...

options.label: Ability to conquer tough obstacles and deliver big wins
Creativity, enthusiasm, and influence on the team
Dependability, patience, and silent dedication
Subject matter mastery, accuracy, and high quality

questions.prompt: 21. When faced with an unexpected crisis or obstacle, my instinct is to...

options.label: Take immediate control and push through with force
Rally people, stay positive, and improvise creative workarounds
Stay calm, hold the fort, and support others through the shift
Step back, assess risks logically, and formulate a clear plan

questions.prompt: 22. Under intense pressure or tight deadlines, I am most prone to become...

options.label: Demanding, impatient, or overly controlling
Overly talkative, disorganized, or scattered
Overly accommodating, quiet, or resistant to conflict
Overly critical, perfectionistic, or withdrawn

questions.prompt: 23. When dealing with workplace disagreement or conflict, I tend to...

options.label: Confront it directly to clear the air and resolve it fast
Use charm, humor, or diplomacy to smooth things over
Yield or compromise to preserve team harmony and calm
Rely on objective facts, policy, and logic to prove the point

questions.prompt: 24. When organizational changes disrupt my usual routine, I...

options.label: Adapt quickly if I see how it gives me more advantage/control
Embrace it eagerly as a fresh and exciting opportunity
Feel uneasy initially and need time to adjust safely
Evaluate it critically to check if the new system is truly logical

questions.prompt: 25. When someone directly challenges my opinion in public, I...

options.label: Defend my position firmly and stand my ground
Reframe the topic with quick wit to keep the audience engaged
Listen quietly to avoid escalating the tension further
Provide evidence, data, and structured points to refute them

questions.prompt: 26. My biggest internal fear in a professional setting is...

options.label: Being taken advantage of or losing control of outcomes
Being rejected, ignored, or losing social standing
Sudden instability, chaos, or hurting team harmony
Making a flawed mistake or being proven wrong/unprepared

questions.prompt: 27. When a plan I designed fails unexpectedly, I...

options.label: Pivot instantly to plan B without dwelling on the past
Reframing it as a valuable lesson and keeping spirits up
Seek comfort in team solidarity and work steadily to fix it
Conduct a deep post-mortem to diagnose exactly what broke

questions.prompt: 28. In situations where I feel overwhelmed with tasks, I tend to...

options.label: Push harder, eliminate distractions, and force my way through
Look for collaborators or talk it through to release stress
Systematically work through one item at a time with patience
Re-organize priorities, build a checklist, and double-check order

questions.prompt: 29. When dealing with difficult or demanding clients, my strategy is to...

options.label: Show strong authority, set boundaries, and drive quick results
Build rapport, diffuse tension with positivity, and charm them
Display calm patience, reassure them, and provide consistent care
Deliver immaculate documentation, facts, and precise answers

questions.prompt: 30. How do you handle feedback that points out a flaw in your work?

options.label: I accept it if it speeds up my results, but prefer autonomy
I prefer it delivered gently and framed around my potential
I appreciate it delivered privately with genuine support
I scrutinize it against facts to verify if it is accurate

questions.prompt: 31. What drives your internal motivation the most?

options.label: Achieving measurable victories and overcoming obstacles
Recognition, social impact, and creative freedom
Security, genuine relationships, and peace of mind
Mastery, accuracy, and producing flawless standards

questions.prompt: 32. If I had to choose a core superpower for my career, it would be...

options.label: Unstoppable drive and courage in high-stakes situations
Magnetic persuasion and inspiring energy
Unshakable loyalty, patience, and dependability
Unmatched analytical depth and high-precision execution

questions.prompt: 33. When reviewing my own work, my primary filter is...

options.label: "Did this achieve the end objective quickly and efficiently?"
"Is this engaging, inspiring, and well-received?"
"Is this helpful, sustainable, and fair to everyone?"
"Is this accurate, compliant, and thoroughly checked?"

questions.prompt: 34. I feel most energized at the end of a workday when I have...

options.label: Crushed a difficult challenge or hit a major milestone
Connected meaningfully with inspiring people and generated ideas
Helped someone out and enjoyed a smooth, productive rhythm
Solved a complex puzzle or perfected a technical system

questions.prompt: 35. When managing projects, my natural tendency is to focus on...

options.label: Setting hard deadlines, driving accountability, and winning
Keeping enthusiasm high, brainstorming, and selling the dream
Maintaining team cohesion, steady pacing, and clear support
Establishing standards, risk registers, and detailed specs

questions.prompt: 36. In terms of professional development, I am most eager to learn...

options.label: Leadership tactics, executive strategy, and negotiation
Public speaking, storytelling, and brand influence
Team dynamics, coaching, and conflict resolution
Specialized technical expertise, domain knowledge, and methods

questions.prompt: 37. How do you view routine, repetitive work?

options.label: I delegate or automate it quickly; I prefer new challenges
I find it boring and prefer dynamic, variety-filled work
I find comfort and stability in predictable daily routines
I enjoy it if it allows me to optimize processes and systems

questions.prompt: 38. When set against a ambitious goal, my mind immediately jumps to...

options.label: How fast we can execute and what stands in our way
Who we can enlist to make this an exciting journey
How we can organize the workload fairly for everyone involved
What the exact steps, metrics, and risks are

questions.prompt: 39. When evaluating a new business opportunity, I prioritize...

options.label: High ROI, competitive advantage, and fast scaling
Innovation, brand excitement, and market appeal
Long-term stability, ethical alignment, and safety
Clear logic, verified financial data, and risk mitigation

questions.prompt: 40. My relationship with perfectionism is best described as...

options.label: Done is better than perfect; momentum matters most
Perfection is secondary to excitement and impact
Perfection is less important than team harmony and steady work
Excellence requires high standards; details make or break quality

questions.prompt: 41. The ideal work environment for me is one that offers...

options.label: High autonomy, authority, and freedom to take initiative
Social connection, creative freedom, and vibrant energy
Predictability, supportive teamwork, and minimal drama
Structure, clarity of expectations, and quiet focus time

questions.prompt: 42. When reading or learning new information, I tend to prefer...

options.label: Executive summaries, bullet points, and key takeaways
Engaging visuals, audio, stories, and real-life examples
Step-by-step guides and practical, hands-on tutorials
Comprehensive documentation, technical specs, and whitepapers

questions.prompt: 43. My approach to managing time and daily schedules is...

options.label: Results-based; I adapt my schedule on the fly to win
Fluid and flexible; I dislike overly rigid schedules
Consistent and steady; I stick to reliable daily habits
Structured and scheduled; I use calendars and precise lists

questions.prompt: 44. What kind of manager or leader brings out the best in you?

options.label: One who sets bold targets and gives me complete freedom to execute
One who is inspiring, supportive, and gives public recognition
One who is patient, consistent, and provides steady guidance
One who is logical, clear, organized, and respects standards

questions.prompt: 45. When contributing to team meetings, I prefer to speak...

options.label: Early and firmly, setting the direction
Often and enthusiastically, keeping momentum high
When asked, or to offer steady, supportive thoughts
When I have verified facts and well-thought-out points to share

questions.prompt: 46. How do you view rules and compliance in everyday operations?

options.label: Useful until they hinder efficiency or growth
A secondary thought compared to innovation and relationships
Essential for maintaining safety, fairness, and calm order
Critical backbone; without them, quality declines

questions.prompt: 47. In a fast-moving brainstorming session, my focus is on...

options.label: Filtering for the single fastest idea that drives results
Generating as many creative, wild ideas as possible
Ensuring everyone has a voice and feels comfortable
Analyzing the feasibility and technical logic behind ideas

questions.prompt: 48. When working under a leader who micro-manages, I feel...

options.label: Restricted and frustrated; I demand space to perform
Suffocated and unmotivated; I lose my creative spark
Uncomfortable with tension, but I will quietly adapt
Fine if their guidelines are logical, precise, and fair

questions.prompt: 49. When completing a long-term project, my greatest strength is...

options.label: Maintaining relentless pressure until cross the finish line
Keeping team engagement and optimism strong throughout
Providing consistent, day-in-day-out effort without quitting
Ensuring zero flaws, meticulous quality, and precise delivery

questions.prompt: 50. Ultimately, my definition of professional success is...

options.label: Achieving power, influence, and high-impact accomplishments
Making a memorable impact, inspiring others, and enjoying freedom
Building a stable, meaningful life with strong community trust
Becoming a true expert who delivers uncompromised excellence

share.sectionSubtitle: Make your reflection easy to pass along.
share.defaultStatus: Works beautifully for LinkedIn-style sharing and quick offline export.
share.copy: I just completed the Northstar DISC Assessment and discovered my behavioral style is “{{badge}}” ({{traits}}). Find your direction here: {{url}}

export.title: Results card ready
export.body: Share a polished reflection that feels executive, clear, and ready for your next conversation.
export.sectionHeading: A polished reflection you can post in one tap.
export.sectionBody: A calm, thoughtful reflection for your energy profile.
export.waitingBody: This will be ready as soon as the anonymous scoring completes.

support.title: Support this work
support.heading: Keep this premium experience moving forward.
support.body: A small contribution helps me keep building thoughtful tools, refined exports, and better leadership experiences. Your support directly powers future features, better polish, and a stronger LinkedIn-ready presentation.
support.series: Follow {{name}} for updates and early access to new improvements:
support.note: Payment support options will be available soon — thank you for helping this project grow.

nextStep.title: Next-step guidance
nextStep.heading: A small next step can make this reflection more useful.
nextStep.intro: Your profile becomes more useful when you use it as a lens for one choice this week.
actions: Pick one meeting where you can slow your pace and ask a clarifying question before jumping in.
Choose one relationship where warmth will matter more than urgency.
Use your strongest style to create a small win for someone else this week.

report.closingBody: Thank you for reviewing this leadership profile summary. For a polished, LinkedIn-ready version and future improvements, follow the work or send feedback.
report.buyCoffeeBody: or share feedback to help keep this project moving forward.
report.badgeBody: A high-clarity behavioral profile designed for coaching, hiring, and team planning conversations.
report.interpretation: Interpretation: {{primary}} leads the profile with a supporting {{secondary}} contribution.

insightPanels.title: Core Strengths & Key Motivators
insightPanels.body: This profile tends to move quickly from intent to action, creates momentum in ambiguous settings, and responds well when the opportunity is directional and timely.

insightPanels.title: Optimal Work & Leadership Environment
insightPanels.body: The best environment combines strong goals, ownership, clear expectations, and enough space to act with conviction while still collaborating with the right people.

insightPanels.title: Behavioral Tendencies Under Pressure
insightPanels.body: Under pressure, the profile becomes more decisive, more results-oriented, and more focused on urgency, alignment, and practical execution rather than extended debate.

insightPanels.title: Actionable Communication & Collaboration Tips
insightPanels.body: Use short, direct updates, frame conversation around outcomes, and pair confidence with a deliberate invitation for others to contribute and stay aligned.

milestone.title: Milestone unlocked
milestone.body: You completed a calm reflection and gave your energy profile a first shape.

dashboard.secondaryNarrative: In a quieter second layer, {{secondary}} also shapes the way you connect, organize, or respond when the pressure is on.

insight.environmentD: High-autonomy spaces where decisions can move quickly and goals stay sharp.
insight.pressureD: Can become more forceful, urgent, or controlling when timelines compress.
insight.communicationD: Direct, concise, and outcome-led; short feedback is often most useful.
insight.environmentI: Collaborative, expressive settings where ideas, energy, and social momentum matter.
insight.pressureI: May become scattered or overly talkative when pressure rises.
insight.communicationI: Warm, narrative-driven, and relational; people respond to energy and clarity.
insight.environmentS: Steady, supportive teams with clear care, calm pacing, and low-friction structure.
insight.pressureS: May seem too accommodating or hesitant when conflict surfaces.
insight.communicationS: Patient, empathetic, and reassuring; trust grows through consistency.
insight.environmentC: Structured, analytical environments where quality, systems, and detail matter.
insight.pressureC: May become overly cautious, perfectionistic, or withdrawn under stress.
insight.communicationC: Precise, thoughtful, and evidence-led; depth and reliability build trust.

D.summary: You tend to move with clarity and conviction when something needs forward motion.
strengths: You help teams make a decision.

I.summary: You bring energy, optimism, and human connection into the space around you.
strengths: You bring warmth to a room.

S.summary: You create calm and trust by being steady, patient, and reliable.
strengths: You bring stability to difficult moments.

C.summary: You tend to notice details, protect quality, and bring structure to complexity.
strengths: You raise the standard of work.
You notice what others may miss.
You create thoughtful systems and clarity.
